expected.reason: Edge stable acknowledges an HTTP Datagram containing only the Quarter Stream ID, but does not expose the resulting zero-length WebTransport datagram to the JavaScript readable stream.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26842019982, Edge job 79151393609, timed out in datagrams.server-empty with datagramLengths=[] after pico_baton logged `datagram, length: 1: 00`.

expected.reason: Edge stable delivered only a partial repeated 1200-byte server datagram sequence before reporting the WebTransport session closed, so this lane does not claim large repeated server datagram delivery.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26899009323, Edge job 79345920128, failed datagrams.server-repeat-large after receiving eleven 1200-byte datagrams with datagramReceiveMin=16, then reported WebTransportError: The session is closed. Chrome passed the same browser run.

expected.reason: Edge stable accepted the repeated one-byte browser datagram scenario far enough for pico_baton to observe one sized datagram, but did not complete sixteen browser-to-server datagram writes before the harness timeout.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26863452355, Edge job 79221931483, timed out in core E2E on the repeated one-byte client datagram row after pico_baton logged one `Received sized WebTransport datagram ... length: 1` and then remained waiting.

expected.reason: Edge stable does not currently claim repeated browser-to-server datagram delivery. The one-byte repeated row delivered only one server-observable datagram before timeout, so the stricter large repeated row remains unclaimed in this lane.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26863452355, Edge job 79221931483, timed out on datagrams.client-repeat-one before datagrams.client-repeat-large could run. The large row depends on the same repeated browser-to-server datagram write behavior.

expected.reason: Edge stable completed the browser-visible incoming unidirectional cancel row, but did not reliably produce a server-observable WebTransport-mapped STOP_SENDING frame in consecutive full core runs. Keep the row active while not claiming the server STOP_SENDING counter in this lane.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26921803310, Edge job 79423629250, and run 26922447204, Edge job 79425550524, both failed reset.browser-cancel-incoming-uni only because server.stopSendingReceived was 0 instead of at least 1.

expected.reason: Edge stable completed the browser-visible incoming bidirectional cancel row, but did not reliably produce a server-observable WebTransport-mapped STOP_SENDING frame. Keep the row active while not claiming the server STOP_SENDING counter in this lane.
expected.evidence: GitHub WebTransportBrowser run 26923501085, Edge job 79428746860, failed reset.browser-cancel-incoming-bidi only because server.stopSendingReceived was 0 instead of at least 1. The same Edge stable lane already has consecutive evidence for the unidirectional incoming-cancel row omitting the same server counter.
